title: Untitled Meeting
notes:
### Candidate Overview

- Victor — Principal Engineer at Verizon (16 years), specialising in enterprise platforms, AI-driven applications, and large-scale distributed systems
- Core languages: Java (many years) and Python (last ~4 years)
- Self-described strengths: technical flexibility across architecture and hands-on development; approachable team lead; SME escalation point for production issues

### Key Projects at Verizon

- B2B e-commerce portal (Verizon’s enterprise ordering platform for large business customers, e.g. Bank of America)

- Oracle as primary database; Cassandra (NoSQL) for downstream data feeds; Informatica for ETL/batch
- Implemented SSO-style “VIP entry” via third-party middleware vendor — allowed enterprise employees to click a link and land directly on the portal without re-authenticating
- Led 5-person production support team; handled customer escalations directly
- Modernisation: migrated from J2EE → Spring Boot microservices using strangler fig pattern

- Zero-downtime migration with phased rollout and automated rollback via CI/CD
- Grafana dashboard built to monitor latency, throughput, error rates
- 30% reduction in CI/CD deployment time achieved
- Data pipeline / AI initiative (database operations tooling)

- Team of ~10; Victor as principal engineer/architect with product manager, engineers, QA, and business stakeholders
- Deduplicated multi-source data (Oracle, Cassandra, Informatica) using Databricks with Medallion framework (bronze → gold)
- Gold-layer data used for AI chatbot and BI reporting (Power BI, Tableau)
- Tuning methods: adaptive query execution, Z-ordering/Delta indexing
- Initial in-house LLM caused hallucination issues → pivoted to AWS Bedrock with RAG/knowledge base and vector database
- Added content filtering guardrails and human-in-the-loop validation for data quality and security
- Solved pain point: database ops team monitoring 250+ servers; ticket resolution was too slow
- Prototyped to production in ~6 months

### Questions Asked

1. Give me a high-level summary of your background and what led you to apply for this role at GHX?

### Role Context Shared with Candidate

- Role reports directly to Mårten; consultative across all 10–12 agile teams (~80 people total)
- Core problem: monolithic architecture needs breaking apart

- Multiple systems/components sharing the same databases — primary pain point
- Heavy reliance on MongoDB as a catch-all; teams need guidance on right tool for right use case
- Stack: mostly Java, deployed on AWS
- AI direction: leaning in heavily — both internal tooling (AWS Bedrock already in use for dev tools) and product initiatives

### Next Steps

- Mårten Engblom

- Review remaining screening interviews before deciding how to proceed
- Follow up with Victor within a few days on next steps
- Victor

- Await feedback; expressed interest in proceeding to team interviews

Chat with meeting transcript: https://notes.granola.ai/t/054016ce-fc05-42aa-978e-0117c1460ff5
